title: SOCI Asset Register currency — evidence every s 18 reportable change is lodged within 30 days
exclusions: Excludes CMDB-level changes that are NOT 'operational information' or 'direct-interest-holder' fields under Part 2 of the SOCI Act (e.g. asset description changes that have no regulatory bearing). Excludes pre-production assets that have not yet been declared in scope of Part 2. Excludes changes made during an authorised correction window (where the entity has formally agreed a back-correction with the Department) tracked in `soci_correction_window_lookup`.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.52.1` archived every hour to `audit_evidence` with `soci_clause="SOCI-s18"`. Every row pins (asset_id, asset_class, change_type, change_detected_at, soci_lodgement_id, soci_lodgement_at, days_to_lodgement, lodgement_status, change_ticket_id, direct_interest_holder_changed). Dashboard `SOCI — Asset Register Lodgement SLA` shows lodgement-aging (0–10, 11–20, 21–30, >30 days), trend per quarter, and an audit-ready CSV export signed by the Head of Critical-Infrastructure Compliance for the annual Rule 10 report to the Department.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Continuously evidences asset-register hygiene: every change in the CMDB that maps to a Part 2 reportable field is matched against an entry in the SOCI lodgement system, the elapsed time is measured against the 30-day statutory window, and every overdue or missing lodgement raises a notable within an hour. The audit_evidence rollup is the artefact filed for any Departmental inspection. Marked 'full' because the UC covers both (a) was the lodgement made and (b) was it made within 30 days; this is the entirety of the s 18 'keep current' duty.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves the Register of Critical Infrastructure Assets is being kept current within the statutory 30-day window after each material change to operational information or direct-interest-holder information.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.52.1` archived to `audit_evidence`, the `SOCI — Asset Register Lodgement SLA` dashboard, and the quarterly CSV export filed with the CIRMP annual report under Rule 10. The lodgement-aging chart, signed by the Company Secretary or Head of Critical-Infrastructure Compliance, is the formal artefact filed for Departmental review.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In the CMDB (ServiceNow `cmdb_ci`), update the `operating_state` or `ownership` (direct-interest-holder) of an in-scope critical infrastructure asset. Do NOT lodge a corresponding entry in the SOCI portal. Within one hour UC-22.52.1 MUST flag the change with `lodgement_status="missing"`, `days_to_lodgement=null`, and route a notable to the Head of Critical-Infrastructure Compliance.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Update the same field, then lodge a corresponding entry in the SOCI portal within 30 days; the lodgement event arrives at `index=soci_lodgement` with the matching `asset_id` and `change_ticket_id`. UC-22.52.1 MUST record the lodgement with `lodgement_status="on_time"` and `days_to_lodgement` between 0 and 30, and MUST NOT raise an alert.

dataSources: ServiceNow CMDB change events (`index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci"`) for asset-attribute changes, ServiceNow `change_request` table for the controlling change ticket, SOCI portal lodgement events (`index=soci_lodgement sourcetype="soci:portal:lodgement"`, HEC-ingested from the entity's regulatory-affairs orchestration), and a KV Store `soci_inscope_assets_lookup` (asset_id → inscope=true, asset_class, business_unit, owner). Optional cross-reference to `soci_correction_window_lookup` for sanctioned back-correction windows.
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742, for AD-derived ownership changes if applicable).
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" earliest=-1h@h
| lookup soci_inscope_assets_lookup asset_id OUTPUT inscope asset_class business_unit owner
| where inscope="true"
| eval reportable_field = case(
    match(field_changed, "operating_state|operational_information|direct_interest_holder|controlling_interest|controlling_corporation|ownership_percent|name_of_responsible_entity"), "true",
    1=1, "false")
| where reportable_field="true"
| rename _time AS change_detected_at
| eval change_ticket_id = coalesce(change_ticket_id, change_request_number)
| join type=left asset_id change_ticket_id [
    search index=soci_lodgement sourcetype="soci:portal:lodgement" earliest=-60d@d
    | rename _time AS soci_lodgement_at, lodgement_id AS soci_lodgement_id
    | fields asset_id change_ticket_id soci_lodgement_at soci_lodgement_id direct_interest_holder_changed
]
| eval days_to_lodgement = if(isnotnull(soci_lodgement_at), round((soci_lodgement_at - change_detected_at) / 86400, 1), null())
| eval lodgement_status = case(
    isnull(soci_lodgement_at) AND (change_detected_at < relative_time(now(), "-30d@d")), "overdue",
    isnull(soci_lodgement_at), "missing",
    days_to_lodgement <= 30, "on_time",
    1=1, "overdue")
| lookup soci_correction_window_lookup asset_id OUTPUT correction_window_until
| where isnull(correction_window_until) OR change_detected_at > correction_window_until
| table change_detected_at asset_id asset_class business_unit owner change_ticket_id field_changed reportable_field soci_lodgement_at soci_lodgement_id days_to_lodgement lodgement_status direct_interest_holder_changed
description: Detects every CMDB change to a SOCI-in-scope asset on a Part 2 reportable field (operating state, direct-interest holder, controlling corporation, ownership percent, name of responsible entity) and matches it against a lodgement in the SOCI portal within the statutory 30-day window. Missing or overdue lodgements raise notables.
value: The SOCI Act s 18 obligation to keep the Register current is the most-cited finding in published Cyber and Infrastructure Security Centre (CISC) regulatory letters — because it is the easiest obligation to fail silently. This UC turns the Register-update SLA into a live operational metric, so the company secretary can demonstrate ongoing compliance on demand rather than reconstructing evidence at audit time.
implementation: (1) Build `soci_inscope_assets_lookup` from the entity's CISC-reported Register; refresh from the regulatory-affairs source of truth weekly. (2) Onboard ServiceNow CMDB change-history feed into `index=itsm` via the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow modular input. (3) Build a small orchestration around the SOCI portal API (or scrape the lodgement confirmation page) and HEC the lodgement events into `index=soci_lodgement`. (4) Schedule UC-22.52.1 every hour; route `lodgement_status=overdue` or `=missing` to notable with severity=high; archive every row to `audit_evidence`.
visualization: (Panel 1) Stacked column: lodgement-aging buckets (0–10 days / 11–20 / 21–30 / >30 days) over the last 365 days. (Panel 2) Sortable table: every distinct (asset_id, change_detected_at) overdue or missing lodgement, with drill-through to the CMDB change record and the controlling change ticket. (Panel 3) 13-month trend: mean lodgement age per quarter. (Panel 4) Coverage gauge: percentage of in-scope assets with at least one lodgement in the last 5 years.

references.title: Security of Critical Infrastructure Act 2018 (Cth) — consolidated as in force
Cyber and Infrastructure Security Centre — Register of Critical Infrastructure Assets

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Bulk CMDB import after acquisition** — a one-shot import of many newly in-scope assets generates many change events whose lodgement window starts at the import date, not at the asset's actual operational change date. Suppression: tag the import batch in `soci_correction_window_lookup` with a correction window aligned to the CISC-approved back-lodgement period.

2. **Non-reportable CMDB field churn** — routine CMDB attribute hygiene (description, support group, vendor contact) flips `field_changed` but does not match the `reportable_field` regex. Confirm the regex covers exactly the s 18 reportable set; widen only on Company Secretary sign-off.

3. **Portal-API outage** — the SOCI portal can have planned maintenance; lodgement events arrive late and appear `missing` until the portal catches up. Suppression: maintain a `soci_portal_outage_lookup` and exclude windows during outage; document the outage and the back-lodgement in the CIRMP annual report.

4. **Out-of-band lodgement (paper / email)** — entities may, by exception, lodge a Register update by signed letter rather than via the portal. The HEC ingest will miss this. Suppression: extend the data feed to include the regulatory-affairs case-management system, not just the portal.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `soci_22_52_1_exceptions` with per-row grants requiring documented IR investigation and a 30-day maximum grant.
mitreAttack: N/A (governance and statutory-reporting workflow — measures Register lodgement timeliness, not adversary detection)
equipmentModels: ServiceNow ITSM (Vancouver / Washington release, cmdb_ci.* tables)
Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, cmdb_ci and change_request inputs)
Splunk Common Information Model Add-on 5.x (Splunkbase 1621, Change data model)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, audit_evidence summary index + notable framework)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), configured with the `cmdb_ci` and `change_request` table modular inputs running every 5 minutes. The add-on's checkpoint state must be backed up daily so a search-head outage does not skip change events.
- Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621); CIM Change data model accelerated if you want the lookups to resolve quickly under load.
- `index=itsm` (90-day retention online).
- `index=soci_lodgement` (7-year retention online — this is statutory evidence under Archives Act and CIRMP r 10).
- `index=audit_evidence` (7-year retention online).
- Lookups:
  - `soci_inscope_assets_lookup` — owned by Regulatory Affairs, refreshed weekly from the entity's CISC Register submission. Columns: `asset_id`, `inscope`, `asset_class` (one of the 22 SOCI critical-asset classes), `business_unit`, `owner`, `last_validated_at`.
  - `soci_correction_window_lookup` — owned by Regulatory Affairs. Columns: `asset_id`, `correction_window_until` (epoch), `case_reference`, `cisc_letter_ref`. Populated only when CISC has formally agreed a back-correction period.
  - `soci_portal_outage_lookup` for portal-API outage windows; optional but recommended.
- A small operator-owned orchestration (Python / Power Automate / Ansible Tower) wrapping the SOCI portal lodgement endpoint, emitting a HEC event for every successful lodgement with `asset_id`, `change_ticket_id`, `lodgement_id`, `lodged_at`. This is part of the SOCI compliance program, not Splunk's responsibility, but the UC depends on it.
- ES notable framework configured with `compliance` or `audit` security domain.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

### ServiceNow CMDB change events

The Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ships a modular input that reads ServiceNow audit tables. To capture CMDB attribute changes:

```ini
[snow://cmdb_ci_audit]
endpoint = https://<instance>.service-now.com/api/now/table/sys_audit
table = sys_audit
query = tablename=cmdb_ci\^ORtablename=cmdb_ci_business_app\^ORtablename=cmdb_ci_appl
interval = 300
index = itsm
sourcetype = snow:cmdb_ci
field_mapping = documentkey:asset_id,fieldname:field_changed,newvalue:new_value,oldvalue:old_value,sys_created_on:_time
```

Validate field extraction:

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| search field IN (asset_id, field_changed, new_value, old_value, change_ticket_id)
```

Volume guidance: a typical ServiceNow CMDB generates 1k–10k audit rows/day depending on tenant size; less than 1 GB/day.

### SOCI portal lodgement HEC feed

The SOCI portal does not natively syslog; you need a small operator script. A working pattern with the CISC portal (treating it as private API):

```python
# A nightly job that lists today's lodgements and HECs them to Splunk.
for lodgement in cisc_portal.list_today_lodgements():
    requests.post(
        "https://<splunk-hec>:8088/services/collector",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Splunk {os.environ['HEC_TOKEN']}"},
        json={
            "event": {
                "asset_id": lodgement.asset_id,
                "change_ticket_id": lodgement.change_ticket_id,
                "lodgement_id": lodgement.lodgement_id,
                "direct_interest_holder_changed": lodgement.dih_changed,
            },
            "time": lodgement.lodged_at.timestamp(),
            "sourcetype": "soci:portal:lodgement",
            "index": "soci_lodgement",
        },
        verify=True,
    )
```

The orchestration is operator-owned. The contract is the HEC event schema above; the rest is implementation detail.

### Asset register lookup

Maintain `soci_inscope_assets_lookup` as a managed lookup, refreshed weekly from the entity's quarterly CISC Register submission. The simplest pattern:

```spl
| inputlookup soci_inscope_assets_lookup_master.csv
| outputlookup soci_inscope_assets_lookup
```

The `_master` artefact is owned by Regulatory Affairs and reviewed at the SOCI compliance steering committee.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

The search is a left-join of CMDB changes to portal lodgements, keyed on (asset_id, change_ticket_id). The `reportable_field` regex is the statutory test: only changes to s 18 reportable fields count. The `lodgement_status` field carries the SLA judgement.

Schedule:

```ini
[UC-22.52.1: SOCI Asset Register currency — evidence every s 18 reportable change is lodged within 30 days]
cron_schedule = 5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.52.1,reg=SOCI,clause=SOCI-s18
action.notable.param.rule_title = SOCI s 18 Asset Register lodgement overdue or missing
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = high
alert.severity = 4
alert.suppress = 1
alert.suppress.fields = asset_id,change_ticket_id
alert.suppress.period = 24h
```

Notable creation only on `lodgement_status IN (missing, overdue)`; `on_time` rows still archive to `audit_evidence` for the positive evidence base.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) In-scope coverage**: every cmdb_ci change with `inscope=true` must arrive in the search input. Cross-check:

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci earliest=-7d
| lookup soci_inscope_assets_lookup asset_id OUTPUT inscope
| stats count by inscope
```

`inscope=true` count vs `inscope=null` proportion tells you whether the lookup is in line with operational reality.

**(b) Portal feed health**: ensure the HEC ingest is alive:

```spl
index=soci_lodgement sourcetype=soci:portal:lodgement earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1d count
```

A flat-zero day during business hours is a feed outage — trigger an internal alert.

**(c) Positive control**: in a non-production CMDB, change `operating_state` of a test in-scope asset; do not lodge. Within an hour, UC-22.52.1 must flag the change with `lodgement_status="missing"`. Capture the notable and the audit_evidence row.

**(d) Negative control**: in the same CMDB, change `operating_state` again, then lodge a corresponding entry via the portal orchestration. UC-22.52.1 must record `lodgement_status="on_time"` and must NOT raise a notable.

**(e) Quarterly reconciliation**: every quarter, the Regulatory Affairs team exports the official CISC Register snapshot and compares it field-by-field against the most recent `audit_evidence` rollup. Differences are escalated to the SOCI compliance steering committee.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `SOCI — Asset Register Lodgement SLA`. Built for the Company Secretary and Regulatory Affairs (auditor-readable).

- **Panel 1, stacked column**: lodgement-age buckets over 365 days. The trend should show consolidation in the 0–10 day band over time.
- **Panel 2, sortable table**: every overdue or missing lodgement in the last 90 days, with drill-through to the CMDB record and the change ticket.
- **Panel 3, 13-month time chart**: mean lodgement age per quarter. The Company Secretary KPI is 'mean age ≤7 days'.
- **Panel 4, gauge**: percentage of in-scope assets with at least one lodgement in the last 5 years. The CISC expects 100% for assets that have undergone any reportable change.
- **Panel 5, top sources of overdue**: which business units / asset classes contribute most overdue lodgements? Drives the Regulatory Affairs improvement plan.

**Runbook for `lodgement_status=missing` notable** (owner: Regulatory Affairs, severity P2):

1. Within 1 business day: confirm the change is in fact reportable (i.e. the field changed maps to a s 18 reportable field) by reviewing the CMDB record with the asset owner.
2. If reportable: lodge a back-dated entry in the SOCI portal with the actual change date. Note the lodgement is late; the portal will record the actual lodgement date for audit.
3. If not reportable: update `soci_inscope_assets_lookup` to exclude the asset OR update the `reportable_field` regex to be more specific. Document the decision.
4. Add the case to the SOCI compliance steering committee agenda; over time, recurring back-lodgements indicate a CMDB-to-Register integration gap.

**Runbook for `lodgement_status=overdue` notable** (owner: Regulatory Affairs, severity P1):

1. Within 4 business hours: lodge in the portal. Late but not missing.
2. Self-report the lateness in the next CIRMP annual report under Rule 10; the CISC distinguishes 'late but lodged' from 'never lodged' and treats the former as a process gap rather than a substantive breach.
3. Conduct a 5-whys on the root cause and feed into the next SOCI compliance steering committee.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**Every CMDB change flags as `missing`** — the portal HEC feed is dead. Check `index=soci_lodgement` for recent events; if zero, the orchestration is broken. Restart and back-lodge.

**Search runs for >2 minutes per hour** — the left-join against 60 days of `index=soci_lodgement` is the slowest part. Consider summary-indexing the lodgement stream to a smaller summary (one row per asset_id with `latest_lodgement_at`) and joining the summary.

**`inscope=null` for all asset changes** — the lookup key is mismatched. ServiceNow `cmdb_ci.sys_id` vs the entity's external asset_id naming. Add an alias column to the lookup or transform in `props.conf`.

**Direct-interest-holder changes never surface** — those are stored in a different ServiceNow table (often `core_company` or a custom relationship table). Extend the modular input to include the relevant table, and extend `reportable_field` regex accordingly.

**SOCI-specific failure modes**:

- **Multiple owners after divestment / acquisition**: in the lead-up to a divestment, a single asset may have a transition period with two direct interest holders. The portal supports this, but the CMDB may not. Pin the transition window in `soci_correction_window_lookup` so the UC tolerates the state.
- **Cross-jurisdictional assets**: an asset that crosses state borders may have multiple owners reported. Resolve to the s 13 'responsible entity' for the asset and use that as `asset_id` primary key.
- **Government-owned assets**: state-government-owned assets that are operated by a private entity report against the operator. Confirm the Register entry attributes the asset to the operator, not the underlying owner.
- **Assets with sub-assets**: a critical electricity generation asset comprises many sub-assets (generation units, switchyards, control systems). The Register entry is at the asset level (NEM-registered unit); operational information changes at sub-asset level may or may not be s 18 reportable. The regex `reportable_field` test must be tuned for the sub-asset hierarchy.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the official paperwork list of our most important systems. Whenever something important changes — like who owns it, or whether it's running — we make sure the paperwork is updated and sent to the regulator within thirty days. We ring the bell if any update is late.
